criteria.description: Many concurrent readers and writers against the same fileID never observe a torn/partial version
criteria.covered_by: TestConcurrentReadersWriters — 12 writer goroutines x 15 CommitVersion calls (180 total) racing 12 reader goroutines each looping NewSnapshot+Read until writers finish, under -race

criteria.description: Every read returns exactly the full content of some committed version
criteria.covered_by: each reader iteration asserts read bytes are an exact member (string equality) of a precomputed, immutable validSet of all 180 possible payloads; failure (t.Errorf) on any mismatch

criteria.description: Test spec: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestConcurrentReadersWriters -race must exist and pass
Genuinely concurrent (go func() + sync.WaitGroup), not a disguised sequential test
criteria.covered_by: 24 goroutines launched via go func(), writerWG/readerWG sync.WaitGroup, no per-goroutine serialization; readers race writers via an atomic done-flag rather than fixed sequential ordering

criteria.description: No race in the test itself between committing and registering valid payloads
criteria.covered_by: validSet + payloads are fully precomputed before any goroutine starts and never mutated during the race (read-only map access, no locking needed)

full package run: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -race -v -count=1 — TestSnapshotRead, TestSnapshotReadNoVersionCommitted, TestVersionWriter (+ subtests), TestCurrentVersionCAS, TestVersionCASWAL (+ subtests) all PASS
go test -run TestConcurrentReadersWriters -race -count=1 (PASS, 7.6s), -count=3 (PASS, 24.3s), -count=5 (PASS, 39.2s) — all clean, zero flakes observed
